scenarios.reply_excerpt: The contents of `docs/concepts/principles.md` describe Reyn's eight design principles (P1-P8).
rubric_met: quotes or summarises content from the principles document
mentions at least one of P1 through P8 or their descriptions
scenarios.notes: File read via chat router worked end-to-end. Both rubric items met.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: I couldn't find any `.md` files under `src/` that mention "judge_output".
rubric_met: mentions judge_output in relation to the found file(s)
rubric_failed: lists at least one file path containing skill.md
scenarios.notes: Grep ran correctly (match_count=0 — no skill.md under src/ mentions judge_output). Rubric item 1 fails because no file path was returned; ground truth confirms no such file exists. Scenario rubric is factually unachievable on this codebase.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: I couldn't find a changelog specifically for the OpenAI Python SDK in 2026. The search results showed changelogs for other libraries.
rubric_met: reports search results or a summary of what was found
references OpenAI or the Python SDK in the response
scenarios.notes: Web search op fired and completed. Reply references OpenAI SDK. Rubric met despite null results.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: I am unable to fetch the content from the provided URL due to a permission denial.
rubric_failed: mentions at least one Python 3.12 feature
reply is a summary, not a raw HTML dump
scenarios.notes: web__fetch tool failed with error_kind=permission_denied (tool_failed event). web_fetch_started never emitted. must_not_emit permission_denied violated (implicit via tool_failed.error_kind=permission_denied). Env-level permission block.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: The command failed to execute because the Python interpreter could not be found.
rubric_failed: does not report an error or permission denial
scenarios.notes: sandboxed_exec_started and sandboxed_exec_completed emitted (events pass). However Python interpreter not found in sandbox — output was error, not '4'. Rubric fails on both items.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: The eval skill failed due to a schema validation error in the postprocessor output. The required properties 'overall_score', 'passed', 'passed_criteria', 'summary', and 'total_criteria' were missing.
rubric_failed: reports whether the skill passed linting or not
mentions error count or warning count
scenarios.notes: skill_run_spawned emitted but skill_run_failed twice. First failure: target skill file not found. Second failure: postprocessor output schema validation error (missing overall_score/passed/etc.). lint_completed never emitted. Postprocessor schema mismatch is a persistent bug.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: I am sorry, but I cannot fulfill this request. The recall tool requires a list of sources to search within, and no sources have been indexed for this agent yet.
rubric_met: either returns relevant documentation chunks about phase rollback, OR clearly reports that no indexed sources were found
does not fabricate phase rollback details without citing a source
scenarios.notes: No sources indexed; system correctly reports no results. Both OR-branch rubric items met. No fabrication observed.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: The artifact at `dogfood/fixtures/judge_phase/sample_artifact.json` is being evaluated... The `judge_phase` skill has finished successfully. The artifact passed all criteria, with a score of 1.0.
rubric_met: references at least one of the two evaluation criteria
scenarios.notes: judge_phase skill ran end-to-end to completion. Postprocessor completed. skill_run_completed emitted. All rubric items met.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: What would you like to name your new skill? [Turn2] Thanks! And what should this skill do?
rubric_failed: the final reply references the skill name provided (my_demo_skill)
OR reports that scaffolding was started or completed
scenarios.notes: Router called describe_action to inspect skill_builder but never invoked it via invoke_action. Turn 1 ended with inline_reply asking for skill name. Turn 2 'my_demo_skill' triggered another inline_reply asking for description — skill_builder never spawned. skill_run_spawned, user_intervention_requested, and user_intervention_received never emitted. Router describe-only attractor: queries skill info but does not execute.

new_findings.title: web__fetch permission_denied blocks web-fetch scenarios
new_findings.description: S4 (web_fetch_url): web__fetch tool failed with error_kind=permission_denied. web_fetch_started was never emitted. This is a persistent env-level permission block that prevents all web-fetch rubric coverage. Same issue likely blocked B46 S4.

new_findings.title: sandboxed_exec Python interpreter not found in sandbox
new_findings.description: S5: sandboxed_exec_started and sandboxed_exec_completed emitted successfully (infra works), but the sandbox environment cannot locate the Python interpreter, producing an error instead of '4'. sandboxed_exec infra is wired but the sandbox runtime is broken for Python commands.

new_findings.title: eval postprocessor output schema mismatch causes persistent lint_a_skill failure
new_findings.description: S6 (lint_a_skill): eval skill run failed with PostprocessorError — postprocessor output missing 'overall_score', 'passed', 'passed_criteria', 'summary', 'total_criteria'. This is the second attempt in one agent turn; both failed. The eval skill postprocessor schema is misaligned with what compute_eval_score returns.

new_findings.title: Router describe-only attractor: skill_builder never spawned in ask_user scenario
new_findings.description: S9: Router called describe_action on skill_builder but never called invoke_action to run it. The multi-turn interaction collected user answers but skill_run_spawned was never emitted. Router falls into describe-only mode rather than executing skills when multi-turn clarification is needed.

new_findings.title: file_glob_grep rubric is factually unachievable: no skill.md under src/ mentions judge_output
new_findings.description: S2: grep ran correctly (match_count=0). The rubric requires listing a file path with skill.md + judge_output, but no such file exists in the codebase. Scenario design flaw — rubric item 1 is unachievable. S2 should be reclassified as I (scenario design flaw) or rubric updated to accept accurate negative result.
